journal_entry.reason: Atlas Van Lines acquired a delivery vehicle (depreciable asset) for $40,000. Payment structure: $20,000 cash (50%) and $20,000 via 2-month promissory note (50%). The vehicle is capitalized as a fixed asset. Cash decreases by the amount paid. Notes Payable increases by the financed amount. No tax mentioned in transaction, so no tax lines added. Debits total $40,000, credits total $40,000 (balanced).

resolved_ambiguities.resolution: Payment method (cash, check, wire) does not affect the journal entry structure — all reduce cash/bank account
Short-term note (2 months) — if interest not stated, convention is to record at face value; interest will be recorded when paid or at period-end if material
Vehicle specifications do not change the accounting entry — all are recorded as delivery vehicle/equipment asset

output.liability_increase_reason: The 2-month promissory note for $20,000 (50% of $40,000) creates a notes payable liability that increases.
output.equity_increase_reason: No equity accounts are affected; this is an asset acquisition financed by cash and debt.
output.revenue_increase_reason: No revenue is generated; this is a capital expenditure for a delivery vehicle asset.
output.asset_decrease_reason: Cash decreases by $20,000 (50% of the purchase price paid immediately).
output.dividend_decrease_reason: No owner withdrawals or dividends are involved in this transaction.
output.expense_decrease_reason: No expenses are reversed or decreased; the vehicle is capitalized as an asset, not expensed.

output.asset_increase_reason: Delivery vehicle (equipment) is acquired for $40,000, representing one asset increase. The vehicle is recorded at its full acquisition cost regardless of the payment method (cash + note).
output.dividend_increase_reason: No owner withdrawals or dividends in this transaction.
output.expense_increase_reason: Vehicle is a capital asset, not an expense. It will be depreciated over its useful life.
output.liability_decrease_reason: No liabilities are being paid off or reduced. The promissory note is being issued (liability increase on credit side), not reduced.
output.equity_decrease_reason: No equity accounts are decreased in this asset acquisition transaction.
output.revenue_decrease_reason: No revenue accounts are involved in this asset purchase transaction.

incomplete_information_checks.reason: Transaction clearly states purchase of delivery vehicle for $40,000, 50% cash ($20,000) and 50% promissory note ($20,000). All necessary information is present.
No tax mentioned in transaction. Vehicle purchases may or may not be subject to sales tax depending on jurisdiction, but no tax information provided.

debit_asset_increase.reason: Accept. One delivery vehicle asset acquired for $40,000 total.

debit_dividend_increase.reason: Accept. No dividend transaction in this vehicle purchase.

debit_expense_increase.reason: Accept. Vehicle is a capital asset, not an expense. Will be depreciated over useful life.

debit_liability_decrease.reason: Accept. No liability is being paid down; a new liability is being created.

debit_equity_decrease.reason: Accept. No equity transaction involved in this asset purchase.

debit_revenue_decrease.reason: Accept. No revenue adjustment in this transaction.

credit_liability_increase.reason: Accept. Promissory note for $20,000 (50% of $40,000) creates a liability.

credit_equity_increase.reason: Accept. No equity contribution in this transaction.

credit_revenue_increase.reason: Accept. No revenue earned in this asset acquisition.

credit_asset_decrease.reason: Accept. Cash paid out is $20,000 (50% of $40,000).

credit_expense_decrease.reason: Accept. No expense reversal in this transaction.

entry.reason: Atlas Van Lines acquired a delivery vehicle (depreciable asset) for $40,000. Payment structure: $20,000 cash (50%) and $20,000 via 2-month promissory note (50%). The vehicle is capitalized as a fixed asset. Cash decreases by the amount paid. Notes Payable increases by the financed amount. No tax mentioned in transaction, so no tax lines added. Debits total $40,000, credits total $40,000 (balanced).

output.decision_rationale: Transaction is clear and complete. Atlas Van Lines purchased a delivery vehicle for $40,000 with mixed financing: 50% cash ($20,000) and 50% promissory note ($20,000). This is a straightforward asset acquisition with partial debt financing. The classifier tuples are correct: 1 debit for asset increase (vehicle), 2 credits for asset decrease (cash) and liability increase (note payable). All amounts are determinable from the transaction text. No tax mentioned, so none applied. Entry balances at $40,000 debit = $40,000 credit. Follows IFRS standards for capitalizing property, plant, and equipment.
